verdict_reason: The candidate's profile is a near-complete mismatch for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. They lack all mandatory requirements related to automotive domain experience, embedded C/C++ proficiency, AUTOSAR, functional safety (ISO 26262), and automotive process (SPICE). The candidate's expertise is firmly rooted in Python, web development, and cloud platforms, which are not the core focus of this highly specialized embedded role.
leadership_indicators: Coordinated with the design team for better UI/UX" (not explicit leadership)
primary_domain: Web Development, Cloud Platforms, Data Analytics, Retail Loyalty Platforms
cleaned_narrative:
The candidate presents as a Python Developer with approximately 1 year and 8 months of documented professional experience, primarily focused on web application development, cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure), data analytics, and microservices. Their expertise lies in backend development using Django, Flask, and FastAPI, frontend with React.js, and database management with SQL and MongoDB. They demonstrate proficiency in CI/CD pipelines, Git, and Kubernetes.

This candidate is not a good fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description explicitly requires extensive experience in automotive embedded software development (5+ years), expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++, strong AUTOSAR knowledge, experience with specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, and familiarity with automotive functional safety (ISO 26262) and process standards (Automotive SPICE). The candidate's profile lacks any mention of these critical automotive, embedded systems, or C/C++ programming skills.

Given the fundamental mismatch in core programming languages, domain expertise, and specific automotive technologies, this candidate does not meet the mandatory requirements for the role.
key_strengths: Strong proficiency in Python development, including frameworks like Django, Flask, and FastAPI, as evidenced by "Created Django APIs," "used Flask with Pickle," and "Developed a FastAPI endpoint."
Solid experience with major cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and related services, demonstrated by "Managed AWS infrastructure (S3, EC2)" and "Securely store PDFs on GCP."
Expertise in modern web development technologies, including React.js, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, as shown by "Designed user-friendly HTML pages using React.js."
Experience with CI/CD pipelines, Git, JIRA, and Kubernetes, indicating familiarity with modern software development practices.
potential_concerns: Core Domain Mismatch:** The most significant gap is the complete lack of automotive embedded software development experience, which is a mandatory requirement of 5+ years.
Programming Language Mismatch:** The candidate's primary languages are Python, JavaScript, and SQL, while the role requires expert-level Embedded C and C++. This is a critical gap.

Specific Automotive Technologies:** There is no mention of AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, CAN/CAN-FD, or automotive-specific tools (Vector CANoe/CANalyzer, DOORS). These are all mandatory or highly preferred requirements.

Years of Experience Discrepancy:** The CV summary states "5 years of product-based applications extensive experience," but the detailed professional experience only totals approximately 1 year and 8 months. This discrepancy needs clarification.
Leadership Experience:** While the role description mentions mentoring junior engineers, the CV does not provide explicit evidence of team leadership or mentoring roles.

detailed_analysis:
SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
The candidate presents as a Python Developer with approximately 1 year and 8 months of documented professional experience, primarily focused on web application development, cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure), data analytics, and microservices. Their expertise lies in backend development using Django, Flask, and FastAPI, frontend with React.js, and database management with SQL and MongoDB. They demonstrate proficiency in CI/CD pipelines, Git, and Kubernetes.

This candidate is not a good fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description explicitly requires extensive experience in automotive embedded software development (5+ years), expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++, strong AUTOSAR knowledge, experience with specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, and familiarity with automotive functional safety (ISO 26262) and process standards (Automotive SPICE). The candidate's profile lacks any mention of these critical automotive, embedded systems, or C/C++ programming skills.

Given the fundamental mismatch in core programming languages, domain expertise, and specific automotive technologies, this candidate does not meet the mandatory requirements for the role.

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
CATEGORY: Total Experience
JD_REQUIRES: 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
CANDIDATE_HAS: Approximately 1 year and 8 months of documented professional experience in Python/web development. The CV summary states "5 years of product-based applications extensive experience" but this is not supported by the detailed professional history. No automotive embedded experience is mentioned.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Core Programming Languages
JD_REQUIRES: Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
CANDIDATE_HAS: Python, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React.js, SQL. No mention of C or C++.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Primary Domain / Industry
JD_REQUIRES: Automotive embedded software development, Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience, Battery management system (BMS) development
CANDIDATE_HAS: Product-based applications, mobile loyalty platforms, data analytics, web development (retail brands). No automotive, embedded, EV, hybrid powertrain, or BMS experience.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Frameworks & Tools
JD_REQUIRES: Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience, Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers, Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools, Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer
CANDIDATE_HAS: Microservices, REST API's, Git, JIRA, Jenkins, CI/CD, Kubernetes dashboard, Jmeter, Rally, Azure DevOps, Elasticsearch, SQLite, PostgreSQL, SQL, MongoDB, FastAPI, Flask, Django, PySpark, Streamlit, Plotly-dash. No mention of AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix, Vector tools, or specific automotive requirements engineering tools.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Architecture & Design Patterns
JD_REQUIRES: AUTOSAR architecture design and integration, Functional safety analysis per ISO 26262
CANDIDATE_HAS: Microservices architecture, API development (Django, Flask, FastAPI). No mention of AUTOSAR or ISO 26262.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Cloud / Infrastructure
JD_REQUIRES: Not explicitly mentioned as a core requirement, but embedded systems often interact with cloud.
CANDIDATE_HAS: Cloud platforms like Amazon Web Services (AWS Step Functions, Lambda, S3, EC2, ECS, SNS), Microsoft Azure, Terraform, Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (Strong cloud experience, but not directly relevant to the core embedded automotive requirements)

CATEGORY: Leadership & Team Management
JD_REQUIRES: Team leadership or technical architect experience, mentor junior engineers
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Coordinated with the design team for better UI/UX" (Software Engineer role). No explicit mention of leading teams or mentoring juniors.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Education / Certifications
JD_REQUIRES: ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification (Nice to Have)
CANDIDATE_HAS: Bachelor of Engineering (B.E) in CSE (Jun 2016 - Jun 2019). No automotive-specific certifications.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Functional Safety & Process
JD_REQUIRES: ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application, Automotive SPICE process experience, MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise
CANDIDATE_HAS: No mention of ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, or MISRA C/C++.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Automotive Protocols
JD_REQUIRES: CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise
CANDIDATE_HAS: No mention of CAN/CAN-FD or other automotive communication protocols.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- Strong proficiency in Python development, including frameworks like Django, Flask, and FastAPI, as evidenced by "Created Django APIs," "used Flask with Pickle," and "Developed a FastAPI endpoint."
- Solid experience with major cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and related services, demonstrated by "Managed AWS infrastructure (S3, EC2)" and "Securely store PDFs on GCP."
- Expertise in modern web development technologies, including React.js, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, as shown by "Designed user-friendly HTML pages using React.js."
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines, Git, JIRA, and Kubernetes, indicating familiarity with modern software development practices.

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- **Core Domain Mismatch:** The most significant gap is the complete lack of automotive embedded software development experience, which is a mandatory requirement of 5+ years.
- **Programming Language Mismatch:** The candidate's primary languages are Python, JavaScript, and SQL, while the role requires expert-level Embedded C and C++. This is a critical gap.
- **Specific Automotive Technologies:** There is no mention of AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, CAN/CAN-FD, or automotive-specific tools (Vector CANoe/CANalyzer, DOORS). These are all mandatory or highly preferred requirements.
- **Years of Experience Discrepancy:** The CV summary states "5 years of product-based applications extensive experience," but the detailed professional experience only totals approximately 1 year and 8 months. This discrepancy needs clarification.
- **Leadership Experience:** While the role description mentions mentoring junior engineers, the CV does not provide explicit evidence of team leadership or mentoring roles.

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: 1 year 8 months (based on listed professional roles; summary claims 5 years)
Seniority Level: MID
Core Technical Skills: Python, Django, Flask, FastAPI, AWS, GCP, Azure, SQL, MongoDB, React.js, Git, JIRA, CI/CD
Secondary Skills: Microservices, REST APIs, Kubernetes, PySpark, Streamlit, Plotly-dash, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Jmeter, Rally, Elasticsearch, Terraform
Primary Domain: Web Development, Cloud Platforms, Data Analytics, Retail Loyalty Platforms
Leadership Indicators: "Coordinated with the design team for better UI/UX" (not explicit leadership)

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
REVIEW
Confidence: 10%
Match Score: 5%

Reason: The candidate's profile is a near-complete mismatch for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. They lack all mandatory requirements related to automotive domain experience, embedded C/C++ proficiency, AUTOSAR, functional safety (ISO 26262), and automotive process (SPICE). The candidate's expertise is firmly rooted in Python, web development, and cloud platforms, which are not the core focus of this highly specialized embedded role.

llm_prompt_used:
You are a senior technical recruiter performing a detailed fitment analysis. Compare the anonymized professional profile against the job description below.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- The CV is already anonymized (all PII removed). NEVER output names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, or company locations.
- If you cannot determine something, state "Not specified" — NEVER invent details.
- Be thorough and evidence-based. Cite specific technologies, years, and project details from the CV.
- Provide a DETAILED category-by-category comparison like a professional recruitment report.
- The Overall Assessment MUST be an original analytical summary you write — do NOT copy-paste text from the CV.

OUTPUT FORMAT (FOLLOW THIS STRUCTURE EXACTLY):

SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
[Write a 2-3 paragraph ORIGINAL executive summary in your own words covering:
- Who the candidate is (years of experience, primary role, key expertise areas)
- Why they are / are not a good fit for this specific role
- One line recommendation
IMPORTANT: This must be YOUR analytical summary, NOT copied text from the CV. Synthesize the information into a professional recruiter assessment.]

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
For EACH major requirement category in the JD, provide a line in this exact format:
CATEGORY: [category name]
JD_REQUIRES: [what the JD asks for]
CANDIDATE_HAS: [what the candidate actually has, with evidence]
MATCH_STATUS: [FULL_MATCH | PARTIAL_MATCH | NO_MATCH]

(Create one entry for each of these categories, adapting to the JD:
- Total Experience
- Core Programming Languages
- Primary Domain / Industry
- Frameworks & Tools
- Architecture & Design Patterns
- Cloud / Infrastructure
- Leadership & Team Management
- Education / Certifications
- Any other JD-specific categories)

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- [Strength 1 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 2 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 3 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 4 with specific evidence from CV (if applicable)]

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- [Gap 1: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
- [Gap 2: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
(If no gaps: "No critical gaps identified.")

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: [exact number, e.g., "9 years" or "5-6 years"]
Seniority Level: [ENTRY: 0-2yrs | MID: 2-5yrs | SENIOR: 5-10yrs | LEAD: 10-15yrs | EXECUTIVE: 15+yrs]
Core Technical Skills: [List top 10 technical skills from CV]
Secondary Skills: [List additional tools, frameworks, soft skills]
Primary Domain: [Main industry/sector e.g., "Automotive Embedded", "Web Development"]
Leadership Indicators: [List concrete evidence: "Led 5-person team", "Mentored 3 juniors", or "None mentioned"]

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
[SHORTLIST | BACKUP | REVIEW]
Confidence: [0-100]%
Match Score: [0-100]%

Reason: [2-3 sentences with specific evidence. First sentence: overall verdict with primary reason. Second: key matching evidence. Third: what tips the balance.]

===== DECISION RULES =====
- SHORTLIST: 80%+ requirements matched with strong evidence. Ready for interview.
- BACKUP: 60-79% requirements matched. Good candidate but has some gaps.
- REVIEW: <60% matched OR unclear/insufficient CV data → human must decide.
- If Confidence <70%, automatically use REVIEW regardless of match score.
- NEVER use REJECT — when in doubt, use REVIEW.

---

JOB DESCRIPTION:
Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS (Must Have):
• 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
• Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
• Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience
• Experience with Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers
• ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application
• Automotive SPICE process experience
• CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise

NICE TO HAVE (Preferred):
• ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification
• Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience
• Battery management system (BMS) development
• Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools
• Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer
• Experience with major OEMs (Mercedes, BMW, Volkswagen, etc.)
• Team leadership or technical architect experience
• MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise

ROLE DESCRIPTION:
We are seeking a senior embedded software engineer to lead AUTOSAR architecture design and integration for next-generation electric vehicle platforms. The ideal candidate will work with cross-functional teams to develop safety-critical automotive systems, conduct SPICE assessments, and mentor junior engineers.

Key responsibilities include:
- Designing and implementing AUTOSAR software components
- Conducting requirements engineering for electric powertrain systems
- Performing functional safety analysis per ISO 26262
- Integrating software on Infineon Aurix multicore platforms
- Leading technical reviews and SPICE assessments
- Collaborating with international OEM customers

This role requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced automotive development environment.
